text:
{
  "title": "External state likelihood",
  "group_name": "external_source_state_likelihood",
  "family": "external_supervised_density",
  "summary": "Represent each student by smoothed health-condition affinity signals implied by matching labeled auxiliary-source lifestyle states, turning source-domain class neighborhoods into compact risk evidence.",
  "depends_on": [],
  "strategy": "Load the auxiliary student_health_dataset_50k file, drop external-only identifier and timestamp fields, and align only the shared project feature columns. Create deterministic state tokens from the external source: numeric features get a missing token plus 8 quantile bins from the external distribution, while sleep duration, BMI, and heart rate also get guideline-style zones using 7-9 sleep hours, BMI cutpoints 18.5/25/30, and resting heart-rate cutpoints 60/100; categorical features use their category or a missing token. From external labels only, build additive-smoothed class-rate tables for each one-way state and for a small set of low-cardinality crossed states: BMI zone x heart-rate zone, sleep zone x sleep-quality x stress level, activity numeric zone x reported activity level, smoking/alcohol x diet type, and hydration zone x activity numeric zone. For each project row, emit the three averaged log smoothed class probabilities, the strongest class-probability margin, the entropy of the averaged class distribution, and matched external support; unseen or missing-heavy states back off from crossed tables to one-way tables and then to global external class priors. Source and method references consulted: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/ziya07/college-student-health-behavior-dataset, https://developers.google.com/machine-learning/crash-course/numerical-data/binning, https://developers.google.com/machine-learning/crash-course/categorical-data/feature-crosses, https://www.cdc.gov/bmi/adult-calculator/bmi-categories.html, https://www.heart.org/en/health-topics/high-blood-pressure/the-facts-about-high-blood-pressure/all-about-heart-rate-pulse, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29073398/",
  "expected_signal": "Because the auxiliary source shares the same feature schema and target classes, smoothed state-level class affinities may expose compact patterns for fit and unhealthy cases that are diluted by the dominant at-risk class, improving balanced class recognition without using project target labels.",
  "risk": "The external labels may reflect a different source-generation process, the auxiliary class distribution is very at-risk heavy, and crossed states can become sparse despite smoothing, so the group may overstate source-specific artifacts or overlap partly with the previous external archetype affinity idea."
}
